description: Drain transparency + drain/evacuate split (always-strict). Closes new ROADMAP P3. Five gaps from operator walkthrough: (1) opt-in STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT confusing — must be unconditional; (2) silent stuck chunks — need impact analysis + UI surface; (3) drain conflates stop-writes-for-maintenance vs full-evacuate-for-decommission — split into two modes; (4) in-flight multipart needs graceful contract; (5) no pre-drain bulk policy fix. Fix: 4-state machine {live, draining_readonly, evacuating, removed} + POST /drain {mode} + GET /drain-impact + <BulkPlacementFixDialog> + redesigned <ConfirmDrainModal> with mode picker + Submit blocked if stuck>0 + <DrainProgressBar> categorized counts + always-strict refactor (remove env/field/chip) + multipart graceful test + 3-scenario smoke. Pre-cycle walkthrough exercise per feedback_cycle_end_to_end.md drove story scope.

userStories.title: State machine 4-state + admin API mode picker
userStories.description: As an operator, I want two distinct drain modes — stop-writes-only for maintenance and full evacuate for decommission — so the action I take matches my actual intent.
acceptanceCriteria: `cluster_state` meta wire shape gains `mode string` field alongside `state string`; legal combinations: `state=live mode=""`, `state=draining_readonly mode="readonly"`, `state=evacuating mode="evacuate"`, `state=removed mode=""`

Migration on read: legacy `state="draining"` rows transparently converted to `state="evacuating" mode="evacuate"` in `meta.Store.GetClusterState` AND written back so the legacy shape doesn't reappear on next read
Memory + Cassandra + TiKV backends implement the new shape; new contract test `caseClusterStateModes` in `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go` exercises every legal + illegal transition
`POST /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain` accepts JSON body `{mode: "readonly"|"evacuate"}` (no default — operator MUST pick); 400 BadRequest if `mode` missing or not in enum

Transitions enforced: `live→draining_readonly` (mode=readonly), `live→evacuating` (mode=evacuate), `draining_readonly→evacuating` (mode=evacuate, upgrade path), `draining_readonly→live` (undrain), `evacuating→live` (undrain — migration stops, no rollback)
Invalid transitions return `409 Conflict` with `code=InvalidTransition` and `current_state` + `requested_mode` fields in the body
`POST /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/undrain` works from both `draining_readonly` and `evacuating` states; flips to `live`; moved-chunks stay on target cluster (no reverse migration)
`GET /admin/v1/clusters` response carries 4-state `state` value + new `mode` field (empty string when state ∈ {live, removed})
`GET /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain-progress` includes the `mode` field
OpenAPI spec at `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` updated
Audit row stamped with action `admin:DrainCluster` + resource `cluster:<id>` + body `{mode}` for traceability
Unit test: every legal + illegal transition pair
Integration test (all three meta backends): legacy `state="draining"` row migrates to `state="evacuating" mode="evacuate"` on first read
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: meta.Store.SetClusterState now takes (state, mode); GetClusterState/ListClusterStates return ClusterStateRow{State, Mode}; legacy `draining` rows migrate to (evacuating, evacuate) on read in all three backends via meta.NormalizeClusterStateRow + best-effort write-back. New contract test caseClusterStateModes covers every legal+illegal combo and the legacy migration path. POST /drain rejects body-less calls (400), unknown modes (400) and refuses (current_state, mode) pairs the 4-state machine doesn't permit (409 InvalidTransition with current_state+requested_mode). POST /undrain refuses from live (409). meta.IsDrainingForWrite collapses both draining states for PUT/drain-cache routing. UI TS client widened: ClusterState/ClusterMode types + isDrainingState helper + drainCluster(id, mode) requires mode (defaults to 'evacuate' so legacy call sites keep migrating; US-004 modal will surface the picker).

userStories.title: Worker scan categorization (evacuating only) + ProgressTracker
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the rebalance worker to categorize chunks on draining clusters into migratable / stuck-single-policy / stuck-no-policy so the operator UI can show what's blocking drain.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/rebalance.ProgressSnapshot` gains three integer fields replacing the flat `Chunks` field: `MigratableChunks`, `StuckSinglePolicyChunks`, `StuckNoPolicyChunks`; total `Chunks()` method derived

Worker scan loop (`internal/rebalance/worker.go::scanDistribution`) classifies each chunk on a draining cluster: migratable = bucket has Placement AND PickClusterExcluding returns non-empty cluster; stuck_single_policy = bucket has Placement AND PickClusterExcluding returns "" (every clustered referenced by policy is draining); stuck_no_policy = bucket has Placement == nil and chunk routed here via class-env routing
Per-bucket breakdown stored: `progressCache[clusterID].byBucket map[string]BucketScanCategory` with `{Category: "migratable"|"stuck_single_policy"|"stuck_no_policy", ChunkCount, BytesUsed}`
Worker scan triggered ONLY when `state=evacuating`; `draining_readonly` clusters get stop-write semantic + status but NO migration scan (saves cost)
`/drain-progress` endpoint returns the three counts + total; `eta_seconds` computed against `MigratableChunks` only (stuck chunks never converge to 0)
`deregister_ready = state=="evacuating" AND total_chunks == 0` (all three categories at 0)
Unit test: scan a fixture bucket with each category configuration; assert counters land in the correct bucket
Integration test (multi-cluster lab): drain cephb evacuate with mixed buckets → progress endpoint reports correct per-category breakdown
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: ProgressSnapshot now carries MigratableChunks / StuckSinglePolicyChunks / StuckNoPolicyChunks (replacing flat Chunks via a derived Chunks() method) + Bytes + per-(cluster, bucket) ByBucket breakdown. CommitScan signature widened to map[string]ScanResult — ScanResult exposes the same three counters + Bytes + optional ByBucket. The rebalance worker now tracks two cluster sets: excludeForTargets (readonly + evacuating + legacy draining; picker exclusion + safety rail) and scanFor (evacuating only — readonly skips migration scan per AC). Per-bucket category is computed once via classifyBucket(policy, excludeForTargets) — constant across chunks because the verdict depends on (policy, excluded) only. /drain-progress handler surfaces the three new int64 fields alongside chunks_on_cluster (total); ETA computed against migratable_chunks only; deregister_ready = total == 0; readonly state returns 'stop-writes mode — migration scan skipped' warning + null counts. OpenAPI spec updated. New unit tests: classifyBucket branch coverage, worker mixed-bucket categorization end-to-end (migratable=2 / stuck_single=3 / stuck_no_policy=1), readonly skip, handler categorized response, handler readonly skip. US-003 consumes snap.ByBucket directly. Learnings: ProgressSnapshot.Chunks is no longer a field — it's a Chunks() method (and same on ScanResult). The picker exclusion set + scan focus set diverged; mixing them silently re-enables move-into-readonly or scans the wrong clusters — use loadClusterDrainSets to obtain both.

userStories.title: /drain-impact pre-evacuate analysis endpoint
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a preview of what would happen if I evacuate a cluster — categorized chunk counts plus suggested policy updates per affected bucket — so I can fix stuck buckets before committing.
acceptanceCriteria: New admin endpoint `GET /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain-impact` returns JSON `{cluster_id, current_state, migratable_chunks, stuck_single_policy_chunks, stuck_no_policy_chunks, total_chunks, by_bucket: [...], last_scan_at}`
Each `by_bucket` entry: `{name, current_policy: map[string]int|null, category: "migratable"|"stuck_single_policy"|"stuck_no_policy", chunk_count, bytes_used, suggested_policies: [{label, policy}]}`
Callable only when state ∈ {live, draining_readonly}; returns `409 Conflict` when state ∈ {evacuating, removed} (use /drain-progress instead)
When state=live: triggers a synchronous one-off scan via rebalance worker scan path (worker doesn't normally scan live clusters); 5-minute cache to dedup concurrent admin requests
When state=draining_readonly: returns cached scan from progressCache if available; cache miss → triggers synchronous scan

`suggested_policies` per bucket — two choices: (i) `{label: "Add all live clusters (uniform)", policy: <draining keys with weight 0, every live cluster with weight 1>}`; (ii) `{label: "Replace draining with <id>", policy: <each live cluster as single-target>}` — generated one entry per live cluster (operator picks)

For stuck_no_policy buckets (no current_policy), `current_policy: null` and suggested_policies offer `{label: "Set initial policy: live clusters uniform", policy: {<each live>: 1}}` + per-live-cluster single-target options
Pagination `?limit=N&offset=M` (default limit=100); sort: stuck_single_policy first, then stuck_no_policy, then migratable; within category descending by chunk_count
OpenAPI spec updated
Audit row stamped with action `admin:GetClusterDrainImpact`
Unit test: stub progressCache + meta.ListBuckets → endpoint produces expected categorized output + suggested policies

Integration test (multi-cluster lab): three demo buckets (split / cephb-only / no-policy) — call /drain-impact on cephb, assert categorized counts match physical chunk distribution + per-bucket suggested policies populated
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: GET /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain-impact returns ClusterDrainImpactResponse {cluster_id, current_state, migratable_chunks, stuck_single_policy_chunks, stuck_no_policy_chunks, total_chunks, by_bucket[], total_buckets, next_offset, last_scan_at}. Synchronous bucket walk (ListBuckets → per-bucket GetBucketPlacement + ListObjects, counting chunks whose Cluster matches the path id). rebalance.classifyBucket exported as rebalance.ClassifyBucket so the categorization matches the live-worker verdict exactly. Per-cluster 5-minute TTL cache (drainImpactCache, drainImpactCacheTTL) keyed by clusterID; lazy-init on Server like hotBuckets. Same cache + scan path for state=live and state=draining_readonly per codebase patterns. 409 InvalidTransition for state ∈ {evacuating, removed}. Pagination via ?limit=N&offset=M (default 100, max 1000) using parseRange — same shape as bucket-references. Sort: stuck_single_policy (0) → stuck_no_policy (1) → migratable (2); within category by chunk_count desc then name asc. suggestedPoliciesForBucket builds one uniform-live + one per-live-cluster single-target entry; uniform forces existing-policy draining keys to 0 (when current!=nil), uses 'Set initial policy:' labels for stuck_no_policy. Audit override stamped admin:GetClusterDrainImpact. OpenAPI spec adds ClusterDrainImpact + BucketImpactEntry + SuggestedPolicy schemas plus the new path under #/paths. Tests cover: mixed-category sort, evacuating→409, draining_readonly→200, pagination + next_offset, cache stability across requests, unknown cluster→400, suggestedPoliciesForBucket no-live edge case + draining-key neutralization. go vet + full go test ./... green.

userStories.title: ConfirmDrainModal redesign — mode picker + preflight + block-if-stuck
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the drain modal to make me pick a mode, show me the impact if I'm evacuating, and refuse to submit if any chunks would be stuck — so I can never accidentally leave data behind.
acceptanceCriteria: `web/src/components/storage/ConfirmDrainModal.tsx` rewritten: top section is a radio mode picker with two options — "Stop new writes (maintenance)" (selected by default) and "Full evacuate (decommission)" — each with a brief description
When mode=readonly: modal skips impact analysis; Submit button enabled after typed-confirm input matches cluster id
When mode=evacuate: modal fetches `/drain-impact` on mode flip; renders categorized impact section showing "X migratable · Y stuck single-policy · Z stuck no-policy"
Stuck rows render with amber background + "Fix N buckets" CTA button opening `<BulkPlacementFixDialog>` (US-005)
Submit button text changes: "Drain (X chunks will migrate)" when stuck=0; "Drain blocked — fix N stuck buckets" when stuck>0; Submit DISABLED while stuck>0
Mode flip refetches /drain-impact; bulk-fix completion refetches /drain-impact via parent prop; counts update live
Typed-confirm input persists from existing implementation (operator types cluster id to arm submit even after stuck=0)

When operator is upgrading from `draining_readonly` (not first-time drain), modal title reads "Upgrade to evacuate" and the readonly radio is hidden (only evacuate option visible — undrain is the way back to live)
Cancel button closes modal without side effects
Bundle size delta ≤ 5 KiB gzipped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: BulkPlacementFixDialog — per-bucket suggestion picker + Apply-uniform-to-all
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to fix many affected buckets in one workflow without clicking through to each BucketDetail individually.
acceptanceCriteria: New component `web/src/components/storage/BulkPlacementFixDialog.tsx`
Opens from "Fix N buckets" CTA in `<ConfirmDrainModal>` (US-004); receives the `by_bucket` list from the parent's cached /drain-impact response

Renders multi-select bucket table with columns: checkbox (default checked), bucket name, current policy summary, category badge (amber stuck / green migratable), suggested-policy dropdown (per bucket — operator picks from suggested_policies offered)
Suggested-policy dropdown default: the first suggested policy from the API (uniform live clusters)

Top-of-dialog "Apply uniform to all selected" toggle: when on, all checked buckets get the same policy (operator picks ONE suggestion from a dialog-level dropdown which overrides individual per-bucket picks)
"Select all" / "Deselect all" buttons

"Apply" button: issues `PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{name}/placement` for every selected bucket with the chosen policy; bulk progress indicator ("3 / 5 done"); failures don't block remaining buckets — collected and toasted at the end
On all-success or partial-success-with-some-applied, dialog closes; parent modal refetches /drain-impact
Cancel button closes dialog without applying anything
Bundle size delta ≤ 4 KiB gzipped
Unit test: rendering with mixed-category buckets + apply-to-all toggle behavior
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DrainProgressBar redesign — categorized counters + mode label + stuck drill-down drawer
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the progress bar on an evacuating cluster to break down what's migrating vs what's stuck so I always know whether I need to take action.
acceptanceCriteria: `web/src/components/storage/DrainProgressBar.tsx` rewritten: renders three sub-counters with color (green Migrating / amber Stuck single-policy / amber Stuck no-policy); total badge sums them
Mode label rendered above counters: "Evacuating" (red text) or "Stop-writes" (orange text); cluster card state badge mirrors the mode

When mode=readonly: bar collapses to a single chip "Stop-writes drain — no migration; reads/deletes/in-flight multipart continue. Undrain to resume writes." + an "Upgrade to evacuate" button + small "Undrain" button
When mode=evacuate AND counters > 0: bar shows live counts + ETA (computed only when migratable > 0; null otherwise so UI hides ETA cell)

Stuck counters are clickable → open `<StuckBucketsDrawer>` (reuse `<BucketReferencesDrawer>` from placement-ui US-006 shape) populated from /drain-progress per-bucket data; each row has "Edit policy" link to BucketDetail Placement tab opening in a new tab
Deregister-ready green chip renders when `state=evacuating AND total_chunks == 0`; chip text: "✓ Ready to deregister (env edit + restart)"
Polling 30s shared query key `clusterDrainProgress(<id>)`
Bundle size delta ≤ 4 KiB gzipped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Always-strict refactor + remove STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT env + in-flight multipart graceful test
userStories.description: As an operator, I want drain to be unconditionally strict and never break my in-flight multipart sessions so I have a hard semantic guarantee.
acceptanceCriteria: `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT` env reference removed from `internal/data/rados/Config`, `internal/data/s3/Config`, `internal/config/config.go`, `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go`, all tests, all docs
`drain_strict: bool` field removed from `GET /admin/v1/clusters` JSON response + OpenAPI spec
"strict" chip removed from `web/src/components/storage/ClustersSubsection.tsx` cluster cards
`RADOS Backend.PutChunks` (and S3 symmetric) ALWAYS refuses fallback to `state ∈ {draining_readonly, evacuating}` clusters → returns `data.ErrDrainRefused`; no env gate
HTTP 503 wire shape unchanged: `DrainRefused` body + `Retry-After: 300` header
Counter relabeled: `strata_putchunks_refused_total{reason="drain_refused",cluster}` (was `reason="drain_strict"`) — hard switch, dashboards must update

In-flight multipart graceful contract: open multipart sessions store cluster id in the multipart handle (`cluster\x00bucket\x00key\x00uploadID` — existing placement-rebalance behavior); UploadPart routes via stored cluster id, BYPASSES `placement.PickClusterExcluding`; Complete/Abort similarly bypass; this means an in-flight multipart finishes on the draining cluster even though new PUTs are refused
New Multipart Init AFTER drain goes through `PickClusterExcluding` → excludes draining → picks alternative or returns "" → 503 if no fallback

Integration test (multi-cluster lab): open multipart on `cephb` (Init + UploadPart x2) → drain cephb mode=evacuate → UploadPart third part → expect 200 OK (graceful) → Complete → expect 200 OK → object readable from cephb
Integration test: open multipart on cephb → drain cephb → Abort → expect 200 OK + parts cleaned on cephb
Integration test: NEW Multipart Init AFTER drain on bucket whose policy has no alternative cluster → expect 503 DrainRefused
Boot-time check: if legacy `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT` env is still set, log WARN and ignore (or fail-fast per Config validation) — implementation choice but consistent across all backends
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke + 3-scenario walkthrough + docs + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As an operator and as a future-maintainer, I need the new drain semantics validated end-to-end (per feedback_cycle_end_to_end.md) against the multi-cluster lab and documented so I trust the cycle is actually done.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/smoke-drain-transparency.sh` covers three walkthrough scenarios end-to-end against `docker compose --profile multi-cluster`

Scenario A (stop-writes drain): create bucket `tx-a` with `Placement={cephb:1,default:1}`; PUT 5 objects; `POST /drain` cephb `{mode:"readonly"}` → assert state=draining_readonly mode=readonly; new PUT to a cephb-routed key → 503 DrainRefused; GET existing object → 200; in-flight multipart Init+Upload+Complete on cephb mid-drain → 200; `POST /undrain` → state=live; new PUT → 200

Scenario B (full evacuate): create three buckets — `tx-split {cephb:1,default:1}`, `tx-stuck {cephb:1}`, `tx-residual` no policy with stale chunks on cephb planted directly via rados PUT for the test rig; `GET /drain-impact` cephb → categorized counts (migratable=K, stuck_single_policy=K, stuck_no_policy=K with K>0 each); attempt drain `{mode:"evacuate"}` → 409 Conflict with stuck breakdown; `PUT /buckets/tx-stuck/placement {cephb:1,default:1}` + `PUT /buckets/tx-residual/placement {default:1}`; retry drain `{mode:"evacuate"}` → 200; wait for worker tick; assert chunks_remaining drops to 0; assert deregister_ready=true

Scenario C (upgrade readonly→evacuate): start from scenario A's state=draining_readonly; `POST /drain {mode:"evacuate"}` → 200; `/drain-impact` returns stuck breakdown; fix + retry → 200 → migration runs to completion
Script EXITS NON-ZERO on any assertion failure; per-scenario log lines so failing scenario is obvious
`make smoke-drain-transparency` Makefile target wraps the script

Playwright spec `web/e2e/drain-transparency.spec.ts` covers UI half of scenarios A + B + C: clicks mode picker + observes impact analysis + opens BulkPlacementFixDialog + applies bulk fix + watches DrainProgressBar categorized counts + sees Ready-to-deregister chip

`docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` "Drain lifecycle" section rewritten: clear separation between "Stop-writes mode (maintenance)" and "Evacuate mode (decommission)"; walkthrough table for each scenario; pre-drain checklist updated to reference impact analysis UI
`docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md` capability matrix updated: BulkPlacementFixDialog row added; strict chip row removed (chip gone in US-007)
Project root `CLAUDE.md` 'Background workers' section updated: rebalance bullet describes scan happens only when state=evacuating
`ROADMAP.md` close-flip new P3 "Drain transparency + drain/evacuate split (always-strict)" → `~~**P3 — ...**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`

`ROADMAP.md` adds follow-up addendum paragraph appended to the closed P3 "Drain strict mode for PUT routing fallback" entry: "STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT env removed in `ralph/drain-transparency` (commit `<pending>`) — drain is now unconditionally strict; strict-chip + drain_strict admin field also removed."
`tasks/prd-drain-transparency.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule)
Manual screenshots of each scenario's key UI states captured + referenced in the operator runbook
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
`pnpm run build` succeeds
`make smoke-drain-transparency` succeeds against the running multi-cluster lab
Typecheck passes
